items.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
items.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
After nearly a month of the hearing that featured testimony from Musk, Altman, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman, former OpenAI board member and mother of several of Musk’s children Shivon Zilis, and a few others, the jury deliberated for a couple of hours before returning with a verdict, deciding to dismiss all charges due to the statute of limitations.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI claimed that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk asked for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation.
People to Know
Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant
Sam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAI 
William Savitt — lead counsel for the defendant
Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge
Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judge

items.titleZh: 埃隆·马斯克与山姆·奥特曼关于OpenAI之争的所有最新动态
items.descriptionZh:
一场备受瞩目的法律对决在埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）与山姆·奥尔特曼（Sam Altman）之间落下帷幕。这场审判关乎OpenAI及其最知名的产品ChatGPT的未来走向。马斯克于2024年提起诉讼，指控OpenAI背离了其创立时的使命——开发造福人类的人工智能，转而将重心放在追求利润上。经过近一个月的听证，包括马斯克、奥尔特曼、微软CEO萨提亚·纳德拉（Satya Nadella）、OpenAI联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼（Greg Brockman）、前OpenAI董事会成员且与马斯克育有多个孩子的希冯·齐里斯（Shivon Zilis）等多名关键人物出庭作证后，陪审团仅商议数小时便做出裁决，以诉讼时效已过为由，驳回了所有指控。

马斯克是OpenAI的联合创始人之一。他声称奥尔特曼和布罗克曼诱骗他向公司提供资金，随后却背离了最初的共同目标——即开发开源、服务于全人类的通用人工智能。然而，OpenAI方面则反驳称，“这起诉讼从一开始就是一场毫无根据且出于嫉妒的企图，旨在扰乱竞争对手”，目的是为了推动马斯克旗下SpaceX、xAI、X等公司的发展——xAI已推出Grok作为ChatGPT的竞品。在诉状中，马斯克曾要求罢免奥尔特曼和布罗克曼，并让OpenAI停止作为公益公司运营。

这场官司的核心人物包括：原告马斯克（OpenAI联合创始人，现为竞争对手xAI的CEO）、其首席律师史蒂文·莫洛（Steven Molo）、家族办公室管理人贾里德·伯查尔（Jared Birchall），以及前OpenAI董事会成员齐里斯（与马斯克有共同子女）。被告方则是奥尔特曼（OpenAI现任CEO）、其首席律师威廉·萨维特（William Savitt）、公司总裁兼联合创始人布罗克曼，以及前首席科学家兼联合创始人伊利亚·苏茨克弗（Ilya Sutskever）。主审法官为伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers）。

从技术背景来看，OpenAI最初作为一个非营利研究机构成立，其核心技术路线是基于大规模神经网络（尤其是Transformer架构）开发生成式预训练模型，最终推出了ChatGPT这一现象级产品。该模型通过海量文本数据的预训练和人类反馈强化学习（RLHF）实现对话能力，代表了当时AI从实验室走向商业化的关键转折。然而，正是这种商业化进程——包括微软的大额投资、成立有限盈利子公司（capped-profit entity）以及将GPT系列模型作为付费产品——成为了马斯克起诉的导火索。马斯克认为，OpenAI违背了将其研究成果免费开源的承诺，实质上沦为微软的闭源利润引擎。

在审判过程中，各方证词揭示了OpenAI内部治理的深刻分歧。马斯克声称自己在2018年曾提议由自己接管公司以避免被微软控制，但遭到拒绝；奥尔特曼则辩称，非营利结构无法支撑AI研发所需的巨额算力与人才成本，转向盈利模式是必然选择。微软CEO纳德拉的证词澄清了微软在OpenAI董事会中的角色，强调微软并不拥有控制权。齐里斯作为当年董事会成员，也参与了早期决策讨论。最终，陪审团并未就案件实质（OpenAI是否真正背离使命）做出判断，而是基于法律程序——诉讼时效已过——直接驳回全部请求。

这一判决对AI行业的影响深远。首先，它并未从实体上阻止OpenAI继续以现有公司架构运营，也未要求其回归非营利模式。其次，它实际上确认了初创公司管理层在演化过程中可以调整使命与商业模式，只要不违反原始合同中的具体法律承诺。对于整个AI领域而言，这意味着“造福人类”这一宏愿在现实商业竞争中的法律可执行性极低——除非创始阶段签订极其严格的约束条款。此外，马斯克旗下的xAI以及Grok产品仍将在市场上与ChatGPT直接竞争，法律上的挫折反而可能促使他在资本与人才争夺上更加激进。从应用场景看，ChatGPT如今已被嵌入微软Office、Bing、Azure等产品线，服务于数亿用户，而Grok则定位为X平台上的实时信息助手——两者的角力将更多转向技术迭代与生态整合。

总体而言，这场审判未能给出关于AI治理终极伦理的判决，但它揭示了一个残酷现实：当“开放、安全、造福全人类”的理想与资本、规模、市场竞争相遇时，后者往往占据上风。未来AI公司的治理结构、公益属性与技术独占性之间的矛盾，仍需通过更清晰的立法或行业共识来解决，而非单一的法庭交锋。

items.title: China says 'world's first' offshore wind-powered underwater data center has entered full operation, houses 2,000 servers — 24 megawatt subsea AI facility uses ocean water for passive cooling and offshore wind for power
items.description: China has begun full operations at a 24 MW underwater AI data center off Shanghai’s coast, using offshore wind power and seawater cooling to reduce energy consumption and improve efficiency
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter China’s “world's first” offshore wind-powered underwater data center (UDC) has begun operations, according to reports from Chinese media. The project, officially launched in June 2025 and completed in October 2025, hit full commercial operation last week, after successful initial trials in February.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowLocated off the coast of Shanghai's Lingang Special Area, the $226 million data center was built and is managed via a direct partnership between the Chinese government and HiCloud Technology (the primary private engineering contractor specialized in subsea data centers), along with state-backed telecom providers like China Telecom.The 24 MW facility houses nearly 2,000 servers (including GPU clusters from China Telecom and LinkWise), and is expected to process artificial intelligence, big data annotation, and 5G infrastructure workloads. Unlike conventional land-based data centers that rely heavily on industrial chillers and large HVAC systems to remove waste heat, the Shanghai UDC uses the surrounding seawater as a massive passive heat sink. The servers are sealed inside pressure-resistant subsea modules deployed roughly 35 meters beneath the surface, where stable ocean temperatures continuously absorb heat generated by the computing hardware.Latest Videos From You may like US startup plans to build data centers inside ocean-based wind turbines, servers water cooled via chilly North Sea Palantir co-founder Peter Thiel backs $140M wave-powered AI data center startup Panthalassa, China’s AI build-out forces a rapid shift to liquid cooling Cooling has become a major bottleneck for modern AI data centers, where dense GPU racks can consume hundreds of kilowatts, converting nearly all of that energy into heat. The underwater design uses surrounding seawater as a passive heat sink, sharply reducing cooling power requirements.Chinese media reports claim the facility achieves a Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) below 1.15, placing it among the most energy-efficient large-scale data centers in operation. Traditional enterprise data centers often operate closer to 1.5 or higher, meaning a significantly larger portion of their total electricity consumption goes toward cooling and supporting infrastructure rather than computation itself.The project also reflects China’s broader push to integrate renewable energy directly into digital infrastructure. The underwater data center is connected to nearby offshore wind farms, allowing a substantial portion of its electricity demand to be supplied directly from renewable generation sources. As AI expansion drives explosive growth in electricity consumption worldwide, countries and hyperscalers are increasingly exploring unconventional infrastructure approaches to address both energy availability and thermal management constraints.However, underwater data centers also introduce substantial engineering and operational challenges. Saltwater corrosion, long-term pressure sealing, subsea cable reliability, and maintenance accessibility remain major concerns. Replacing failed hardware is considerably more complex than in conventional facilities, where technicians can physically access racks within minutes. Operators therefore rely heavily on sealed modular designs, remote monitoring systems, and highly redundant infrastructure intended to minimize the need for physical intervention.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe Shanghai project follows earlier experimental efforts such as Microsoft’s Project Natick, which tested submerged data center capsules off the coasts of Scotland and California. Microsoft ultimately discontinued the program commercially, but the trials demonstrated that underwater deployments could achieve lower hardware failure rates.Offshore-powered, ocean-cooled data center projects are continuing to emerge worldwide as AI infrastructure power and cooling demands continue to soar. Last month, we reported on a Peter Thiel-backed startup, Panthalassa, which is developing wave-powered floating data centers designed to operate far offshore using ocean water for passive cooling while drawing electricity from onboard renewable energy systems. items.titleZh: 中国称“全球首个”海上风电驱动水下数据中心全面运营，容纳2000台服务器——24兆瓦水下AI设施利用海水被动冷却及海上风电供电
items.descriptionZh:
中国上海外海的世界首个海上风电驱动海底数据中心（UDC）已正式进入商业运营阶段。据中国媒体报道，该项目于2025年6月启动建设，同年10月完工，在2月成功完成初步试验后，于上周达到全面商业运行状态。

这座耗资2.26亿美元的数据中心位于上海临港新片区外海，由中国政府与海底数据中心专业承包商HiCloud Technology（主要私营工程承包商）以及中国电信等国有电信运营商直接合作建设并管理。

该设施容量为24兆瓦，部署了近2000台服务器（包括来自中国电信和LinkWise的GPU集群），预计将处理人工智能、大数据标注和5G基础设施等工作负载。与依赖工业冷水机组和大型暖通空调系统来散热的地面数据中心不同，上海海底数据中心利用周围海水作为巨大的被动散热器。服务器被密封在耐压深海模块中，部署在水下约35米深处，此处稳定的海洋温度持续吸收计算硬件产生的热量。

冷却已成为现代AI数据中心的主要瓶颈，密集的GPU机架功耗可达数百千瓦，几乎将所有电能转化为热量。海底设计利用周围海水作为被动散热器，大幅降低了冷却耗电量。据中国媒体报道，该设施的电源使用效率（PUE）低于1.15，使其成为目前运行中能效最高的大型数据中心之一。相比之下，传统企业数据中心的PUE通常在1.5甚至更高，意味着相当大比例的电力消耗用于冷却和配套基础设施，而非实际的计算任务。

该项目还体现了中国将可再生能源直接融入数字基础设施的广泛努力。海底数据中心与附近的近海风电场相连，相当一部分电力需求可直接来自可再生发电来源。随着AI扩张推动全球电力消耗迅猛增长，各国和超大规模云服务商正越来越多地探索非传统基础设施方案，以解决能源可用性和热管理难题。

然而，海底数据中心也带来了重大的工程和运营挑战。海水腐蚀、长期耐压密封、海底电缆可靠性以及维护可达性仍是主要问题。更换故障硬件比传统设施复杂得多——在传统设施中，技术人员几分钟内即可物理接触机架。因此，运营商高度依赖密封模块化设计、远程监控系统和高度冗余的基础设施，以最大限度地减少物理干预需求。

上海项目之前已有类似的实验性尝试，例如微软的Project Natick，该项目曾在苏格兰和加利福尼亚外海测试水下数据中心胶囊。微软最终商业上停止了该项目，但试验表明，水下部署可降低硬件故障率。

随着AI基础设施的功率和冷却需求持续飙升，由海上风电驱动、海水冷却的数据中心项目正在全球范围内不断涌现。上个月，我们报道了一家由Peter Thiel支持的初创公司Panthalassa，该公司正在开发波浪能驱动的浮式数据中心，旨在远离海岸运行，利用海水被动冷却，同时从船上可再生能源系统获取电力。

items.title: Enthusiast crams reversed-engineered PS2 into a handheld, designs custom motherboard — bespoke "PlayStation 2 Portable" pairs modern features with original silicon
items.description: What if you wanted to play PS2 games on the go but didn't want to emulate them? Meet the PS2 Portable: an open-source handheld pieced together with a custom motherboard featuring original PS2 silicon and modern niceties such as hall-effect joysticks. It can play any PS2 game natively for up to 4.5 hours on a single charge, which happens via USB-C.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A modder by the name of tschicki has built a custom "PlayStation 2 Portable" from scratch, powered by a reverse-engineered motherboard that features original PS2 silicon. The hardware sits inside a 3D-printed shell with proper controls, USB-PD charging, and a bespoke cooling solution. The project is entirely open-source and available on GitHub for anyone to follow along with, if you have the right skills and patience required.Work on the PS2 Portable actually began way back in 2022, but it was only made public last year. The four-year development period really shows when you take a look at how polished the finished product is. The design is inspired by modern handhelds such as the ROG Ally; it has an ergonomic shape with large grips that house two 5000mAh batteries to enable up to 4.5 hours of playtime on a single charge.On the front, there's an asymmetric control scheme that actually features hall-effect joysticks and face buttons from the PS Vita 2000 model. There's a blue accent piece running along the top, enveloping the triggers and the USB-C port, providing some variety in an otherwise minimalist aesthetic. You'll also find vents up there for exhaust. The rear is largely plain with a few threaded screws and a cutout with a grill for intake.Latest Videos FromImage 1 of 2(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)The entire assembly is broken down into just two parts: a top half and a bottom half that screw into each other. The cooling is handled by a custom heatsink solution that uses the fan from the Switch Lite to keep the ICs under control.This transitions us nicely into the actual crux of the project. The modder took six original ICs from the SCPH-7900x or SCPH-9000x PS2 models and put them on a custom motherboard.Built upon the existing work of community veterans who have mapped out PS2 mainboards over the years, Tschicki reverse-engineered the entire circuitry of the console. They were then able to manually draw up a new motherboard layout that integrates the aforementioned original chips with completely new traces, video routing, and power management.The donor ICs handle the bulk of the processing, such as the Emotion Engine (EE) and the Graphics Synthesizer (GS), while RP2040 microcontrollers are responsible for secondary tasks like thermals, controls, sound, and more. The modder used a custom FPGA-based video processor to get direct video out from the GS instead of going through an analog-to-digital converter that'd otherwise degrade the quality.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)As such, the PS2 portable supports many resolutions, but it's wired up to a 5" 480x800p IPS LCD running at 60 Hz. To ensure the controls work on this screen, the RP2040 is utilized to convert data from the buttons and joysticks into native DualShock 2 signals, with full rumble support. Another RP2040 monitors the battery and controls power, allowing for fast charging or "charge and play" at 5V, 9V, or 12V.Finally, to actually use the console, tschicki customized the original boot ROM to directly launch into homebrew, from where you can use the two microSD card slots present on the device to access your games. One slot hooks directly to the PS2 Memory Card's data lines and tricks the system into thinking one of those bulky 8MB Memory Cards is plugged in. You can use this for boot files and game saves, for instance.The other slot bypasses the PS2's memory controller entirely and uses the open-source MX4SIO routing standard that doesn't try to emulate a memory card. Instead, it relies on custom homebrew apps running on the console to talk directly to the SD card's native storage interface. Once the custom MX4SIO driver is initialized, you can load up any game you want, which means this slot is better for library storage.This is possible because the BIOS chip from the actual PS2 is one of the six ICs lifted from the original console, along with the CPU+GPU, the RAM (two separate chips), the I/O processor, and the SP2 audio processor. Anyhow, after the games are loaded, the handheld can play PS2 titles natively via custom software like OPL (Open PS2 Loader) or NHDDL, and supports PS1 games via DKWDRV. There is no emulation happening here.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)Because of the sheer stature of custom modding work done, the PS2 Portable is one of the most impressive community projects in recent times. The fact that everything from the 3D printer files (STLs), the PCB schematics, the FPGA code, and the software firmware is open source is just the cherry on top. You can build your own identical handheld, but it's not going to be easy, and Tschicki even warns against it. All of the relevant resources are available on GitHub, including the BOM (bill of materials) for the mechanical assembly and each PCBA. There's a 10-part documentation with steps you can follow, but they're incredibly detailed and only meant for experts with high-level soldering and programming knowledge. Do not attempt this as a fun DIY project, but then again, curiosity killed the cat.Now, technically speaking, a portable PS2 already exists — it's called the PlayStation Portable (PSP), and it was one of the most successful consoles of all time. But it doesn't have the PS2's massive catalog of classics, so there's an argument to be made there. That being said, emulation has come a long way, and your phone can even play AAA PC games now, so stick to emulation if you're only after the nostalgia and not the microsoldering anguish. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 爱好者将逆向工程版PS2塞进掌机，设计定制主板——定制的“PlayStation 2 Portable”将现代功能与原始芯片相结合
items.descriptionZh:
一位名为tschicki的Modder从零打造了一台"PlayStation 2 Portable"掌机，其核心是一块经过逆向工程设计的定制主板，搭载了原装PS2芯片。整机包裹在3D打印外壳中，配备完整的操控方案、USB-PD充电以及独特的散热系统。该项目完全开源，所有文件已上传至GitHub，供有足够技能和耐心的玩家参考制作。

这台PS2掌机的开发始于2022年，但直到去年才公开亮相。四年的研发周期从最终成品的精致程度可见一斑。设计灵感源自ROG Ally等现代掌机，采用符合人体工学的造型，两侧大型握把内各塞入一块5000mAh电池，单次充电可提供长达4.5小时的游戏时间。正面采用非对称布局，配备了霍尔效应摇杆和来自PS Vita 2000型号的按键模组。顶部贯穿一条蓝色装饰件，包裹着扳机键和USB-C接口，在极简美学中增添了一抹亮色。上方还设有排风散热口。背面较为平整，有几颗螺纹螺丝和一个进风格栅开孔。

整机仅由上下两半外壳通过螺丝组装而成。散热方案采用定制的散热片，结合Switch Lite的散热风扇来控制芯片温度。这项设计自然引出了项目的核心：Modder从SCPH-7900x或SCPH-9000x型号的PS2上拆下了六颗原始IC，将它们重新安置在一块定制主板上。

基于社区资深玩家多年绘制PS2主板布局的成果，Tschicki对整个PS2电路进行了逆向工程，然后手动绘制出新的主板布局，将这些原装芯片与全新的走线、视频路由和电源管理电路整合在一起。捐赠的IC负责大部分核心处理任务，例如情感引擎（EE）和图形合成器（GS），而RP2040微控制器则负责次级任务，如散热、控制、音频等。Modder还使用基于FPGA的定制视频处理器直接从GS输出视频信号，避免了传统模拟转数字转换器带来的画质损失。

PS2掌机支持多种分辨率，但屏幕是一块5英寸480×800p的IPS LCD面板，刷新率60Hz。为确保操控与屏幕协同工作，RP2040将按键和摇杆的数据转换为原生的DualShock 2信号，并完整支持震动反馈。另一颗RP2040负责监控电池并控制电源，支持5V、9V或12V的快充以及边充边玩功能。

最后，为了让机器实际运行，Tschicki定制了原始启动ROM，直接引导进入自制系统（homebrew）。设备配备了两个microSD卡槽：一个卡槽直接连接到PS2记忆卡的数据线，让系统误以为插入了传统8MB记忆卡，可用于存放引导文件和游戏存档；另一个卡槽则完全绕过PS2的内存控制器，采用开源的MX4SIO路由标准——它不试图模拟记忆卡，而是依赖运行在主机上的自制软件直接与SD卡的原生存储接口通信。一旦初始化了定制MX4SIO驱动，即可加载任何想要的游戏，因此这个卡槽更适合存放游戏库。

由于从原装PS2上拆下的六颗IC包括CPU+GPU、两块RAM芯片、I/O处理器、SP2音频处理器以及BIOS芯片，游戏加载后，这台掌机可通过OPL（Open PS2 Loader）或NHDDL等定制软件原生运行PS2游戏，并通过DKWDRV支持PS1游戏——这是一台不依赖模拟器的纯硬件掌机。

鉴于如此庞大的自定义改造工作量，这台PS2 Portable堪称近年来最令人印象深刻的社区项目之一。而所有3D打印文件（STL）、PCB原理图、FPGA代码以及软件固件全部开源，更是锦上添花。你可以复刻一台完全相同的手持设备，但这绝非易事——Tschicki甚至警告不要轻易尝试。所有相关资源（包括机械组装和每块PCBA的物料清单BOM）都已上传至GitHub，同时附有10个步骤的详细文档，但内容极其专业，仅适合拥有高阶焊接和编程知识的专家。请不要将其视为一个有趣的DIY项目——好奇会害死猫。

从技术上说，便携PS2早已存在——那就是PlayStation Portable（PSP），史上最成功的游戏机之一。但PSP无法运行PS2庞大的经典游戏库，因此这台掌机的价值不言而喻。当然，模拟技术也已今非昔比，连手机都能玩AAA级PC游戏了。如果你只是追求怀旧而非微焊接的痛苦，还是乖乖用模拟器吧。

history.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
history.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
After nearly a month of the hearing that featured testimony from Musk, Altman, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman, former OpenAI board member and mother of several of Musk’s children Shivon Zilis, and a few others, the jury deliberated for a couple of hours before returning with a verdict, deciding to dismiss all charges due to the statute of limitations.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI claimed that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk asked for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation.

Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant
Sam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAI 
William Savitt — lead counsel for the defendant
Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge

history.content: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead.After nearly a month of the hearing that featured testimony from Musk, Altman, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman, former OpenAI board member and mother of several of Musk’s children Shivon Zilis, and a few others, the jury deliberated for a couple of hours before returning with a verdict, deciding to dismiss all charges due to the statute of limitations.Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI claimed that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.In his lawsuit, Musk asked for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation.People to KnowPlaintiffElon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAISteven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiffJared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family officeShivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with MuskDefendantSam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAIWilliam Savitt — lead counsel for the defendantGreg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounderIlya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounderJudgeYvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judgeHere’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:
history.author: Elizabeth Lopatto
history.titleZh: 埃隆·马斯克与山姆·奥特曼关于OpenAI之争的所有最新动态
history.descriptionZh:
一场备受瞩目的法律对决在埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）与山姆·奥尔特曼（Sam Altman）之间落下帷幕。这场审判关乎OpenAI及其最知名的产品ChatGPT的未来走向。马斯克于2024年提起诉讼，指控OpenAI背离了其创立时的使命——开发造福人类的人工智能，转而将重心放在追求利润上。经过近一个月的听证，包括马斯克、奥尔特曼、微软CEO萨提亚·纳德拉（Satya Nadella）、OpenAI联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼（Greg Brockman）、前OpenAI董事会成员且与马斯克育有多个孩子的希冯·齐里斯（Shivon Zilis）等多名关键人物出庭作证后，陪审团仅商议数小时便做出裁决，以诉讼时效已过为由，驳回了所有指控。

马斯克是OpenAI的联合创始人之一。他声称奥尔特曼和布罗克曼诱骗他向公司提供资金，随后却背离了最初的共同目标——即开发开源、服务于全人类的通用人工智能。然而，OpenAI方面则反驳称，“这起诉讼从一开始就是一场毫无根据且出于嫉妒的企图，旨在扰乱竞争对手”，目的是为了推动马斯克旗下SpaceX、xAI、X等公司的发展——xAI已推出Grok作为ChatGPT的竞品。在诉状中，马斯克曾要求罢免奥尔特曼和布罗克曼，并让OpenAI停止作为公益公司运营。

这场官司的核心人物包括：原告马斯克（OpenAI联合创始人，现为竞争对手xAI的CEO）、其首席律师史蒂文·莫洛（Steven Molo）、家族办公室管理人贾里德·伯查尔（Jared Birchall），以及前OpenAI董事会成员齐里斯（与马斯克有共同子女）。被告方则是奥尔特曼（OpenAI现任CEO）、其首席律师威廉·萨维特（William Savitt）、公司总裁兼联合创始人布罗克曼，以及前首席科学家兼联合创始人伊利亚·苏茨克弗（Ilya Sutskever）。主审法官为伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers）。

从技术背景来看，OpenAI最初作为一个非营利研究机构成立，其核心技术路线是基于大规模神经网络（尤其是Transformer架构）开发生成式预训练模型，最终推出了ChatGPT这一现象级产品。该模型通过海量文本数据的预训练和人类反馈强化学习（RLHF）实现对话能力，代表了当时AI从实验室走向商业化的关键转折。然而，正是这种商业化进程——包括微软的大额投资、成立有限盈利子公司（capped-profit entity）以及将GPT系列模型作为付费产品——成为了马斯克起诉的导火索。马斯克认为，OpenAI违背了将其研究成果免费开源的承诺，实质上沦为微软的闭源利润引擎。

在审判过程中，各方证词揭示了OpenAI内部治理的深刻分歧。马斯克声称自己在2018年曾提议由自己接管公司以避免被微软控制，但遭到拒绝；奥尔特曼则辩称，非营利结构无法支撑AI研发所需的巨额算力与人才成本，转向盈利模式是必然选择。微软CEO纳德拉的证词澄清了微软在OpenAI董事会中的角色，强调微软并不拥有控制权。齐里斯作为当年董事会成员，也参与了早期决策讨论。最终，陪审团并未就案件实质（OpenAI是否真正背离使命）做出判断，而是基于法律程序——诉讼时效已过——直接驳回全部请求。

这一判决对AI行业的影响深远。首先，它并未从实体上阻止OpenAI继续以现有公司架构运营，也未要求其回归非营利模式。其次，它实际上确认了初创公司管理层在演化过程中可以调整使命与商业模式，只要不违反原始合同中的具体法律承诺。对于整个AI领域而言，这意味着“造福人类”这一宏愿在现实商业竞争中的法律可执行性极低——除非创始阶段签订极其严格的约束条款。此外，马斯克旗下的xAI以及Grok产品仍将在市场上与ChatGPT直接竞争，法律上的挫折反而可能促使他在资本与人才争夺上更加激进。从应用场景看，ChatGPT如今已被嵌入微软Office、Bing、Azure等产品线，服务于数亿用户，而Grok则定位为X平台上的实时信息助手——两者的角力将更多转向技术迭代与生态整合。

总体而言，这场审判未能给出关于AI治理终极伦理的判决，但它揭示了一个残酷现实：当“开放、安全、造福全人类”的理想与资本、规模、市场竞争相遇时，后者往往占据上风。未来AI公司的治理结构、公益属性与技术独占性之间的矛盾，仍需通过更清晰的立法或行业共识来解决，而非单一的法庭交锋。

history.title: China says 'world's first' offshore wind-powered underwater data center has entered full operation, houses 2,000 servers — 24 megawatt subsea AI facility uses ocean water for passive cooling and offshore wind for power
history.description: China has begun full operations at a 24 MW underwater AI data center off Shanghai’s coast, using offshore wind power and seawater cooling to reduce energy consumption and improve efficiency
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter China’s “world's first” offshore wind-powered underwater data center (UDC) has begun operations, according to reports from Chinese media. The project, officially launched in June 2025 and completed in October 2025, hit full commercial operation last week, after successful initial trials in February.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowLocated off the coast of Shanghai's Lingang Special Area, the $226 million data center was built and is managed via a direct partnership between the Chinese government and HiCloud Technology (the primary private engineering contractor specialized in subsea data centers), along with state-backed telecom providers like China Telecom.The 24 MW facility houses nearly 2,000 servers (including GPU clusters from China Telecom and LinkWise), and is expected to process artificial intelligence, big data annotation, and 5G infrastructure workloads. Unlike conventional land-based data centers that rely heavily on industrial chillers and large HVAC systems to remove waste heat, the Shanghai UDC uses the surrounding seawater as a massive passive heat sink. The servers are sealed inside pressure-resistant subsea modules deployed roughly 35 meters beneath the surface, where stable ocean temperatures continuously absorb heat generated by the computing hardware.Latest Videos From You may like US startup plans to build data centers inside ocean-based wind turbines, servers water cooled via chilly North Sea Palantir co-founder Peter Thiel backs $140M wave-powered AI data center startup Panthalassa, China’s AI build-out forces a rapid shift to liquid cooling Cooling has become a major bottleneck for modern AI data centers, where dense GPU racks can consume hundreds of kilowatts, converting nearly all of that energy into heat. The underwater design uses surrounding seawater as a passive heat sink, sharply reducing cooling power requirements.Chinese media reports claim the facility achieves a Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) below 1.15, placing it among the most energy-efficient large-scale data centers in operation. Traditional enterprise data centers often operate closer to 1.5 or higher, meaning a significantly larger portion of their total electricity consumption goes toward cooling and supporting infrastructure rather than computation itself.The project also reflects China’s broader push to integrate renewable energy directly into digital infrastructure. The underwater data center is connected to nearby offshore wind farms, allowing a substantial portion of its electricity demand to be supplied directly from renewable generation sources. As AI expansion drives explosive growth in electricity consumption worldwide, countries and hyperscalers are increasingly exploring unconventional infrastructure approaches to address both energy availability and thermal management constraints.However, underwater data centers also introduce substantial engineering and operational challenges. Saltwater corrosion, long-term pressure sealing, subsea cable reliability, and maintenance accessibility remain major concerns. Replacing failed hardware is considerably more complex than in conventional facilities, where technicians can physically access racks within minutes. Operators therefore rely heavily on sealed modular designs, remote monitoring systems, and highly redundant infrastructure intended to minimize the need for physical intervention.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe Shanghai project follows earlier experimental efforts such as Microsoft’s Project Natick, which tested submerged data center capsules off the coasts of Scotland and California. Microsoft ultimately discontinued the program commercially, but the trials demonstrated that underwater deployments could achieve lower hardware failure rates.Offshore-powered, ocean-cooled data center projects are continuing to emerge worldwide as AI infrastructure power and cooling demands continue to soar. Last month, we reported on a Peter Thiel-backed startup, Panthalassa, which is developing wave-powered floating data centers designed to operate far offshore using ocean water for passive cooling while drawing electricity from onboard renewable energy systems. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 中国称“全球首个”海上风电驱动水下数据中心全面运营，容纳2000台服务器——24兆瓦水下AI设施利用海水被动冷却及海上风电供电
history.descriptionZh:
中国上海外海的世界首个海上风电驱动海底数据中心（UDC）已正式进入商业运营阶段。据中国媒体报道，该项目于2025年6月启动建设，同年10月完工，在2月成功完成初步试验后，于上周达到全面商业运行状态。

这座耗资2.26亿美元的数据中心位于上海临港新片区外海，由中国政府与海底数据中心专业承包商HiCloud Technology（主要私营工程承包商）以及中国电信等国有电信运营商直接合作建设并管理。

该设施容量为24兆瓦，部署了近2000台服务器（包括来自中国电信和LinkWise的GPU集群），预计将处理人工智能、大数据标注和5G基础设施等工作负载。与依赖工业冷水机组和大型暖通空调系统来散热的地面数据中心不同，上海海底数据中心利用周围海水作为巨大的被动散热器。服务器被密封在耐压深海模块中，部署在水下约35米深处，此处稳定的海洋温度持续吸收计算硬件产生的热量。

冷却已成为现代AI数据中心的主要瓶颈，密集的GPU机架功耗可达数百千瓦，几乎将所有电能转化为热量。海底设计利用周围海水作为被动散热器，大幅降低了冷却耗电量。据中国媒体报道，该设施的电源使用效率（PUE）低于1.15，使其成为目前运行中能效最高的大型数据中心之一。相比之下，传统企业数据中心的PUE通常在1.5甚至更高，意味着相当大比例的电力消耗用于冷却和配套基础设施，而非实际的计算任务。

该项目还体现了中国将可再生能源直接融入数字基础设施的广泛努力。海底数据中心与附近的近海风电场相连，相当一部分电力需求可直接来自可再生发电来源。随着AI扩张推动全球电力消耗迅猛增长，各国和超大规模云服务商正越来越多地探索非传统基础设施方案，以解决能源可用性和热管理难题。

然而，海底数据中心也带来了重大的工程和运营挑战。海水腐蚀、长期耐压密封、海底电缆可靠性以及维护可达性仍是主要问题。更换故障硬件比传统设施复杂得多——在传统设施中，技术人员几分钟内即可物理接触机架。因此，运营商高度依赖密封模块化设计、远程监控系统和高度冗余的基础设施，以最大限度地减少物理干预需求。

上海项目之前已有类似的实验性尝试，例如微软的Project Natick，该项目曾在苏格兰和加利福尼亚外海测试水下数据中心胶囊。微软最终商业上停止了该项目，但试验表明，水下部署可降低硬件故障率。

随着AI基础设施的功率和冷却需求持续飙升，由海上风电驱动、海水冷却的数据中心项目正在全球范围内不断涌现。上个月，我们报道了一家由Peter Thiel支持的初创公司Panthalassa，该公司正在开发波浪能驱动的浮式数据中心，旨在远离海岸运行，利用海水被动冷却，同时从船上可再生能源系统获取电力。

history.title: Enthusiast crams reversed-engineered PS2 into a handheld, designs custom motherboard — bespoke "PlayStation 2 Portable" pairs modern features with original silicon
history.description: What if you wanted to play PS2 games on the go but didn't want to emulate them? Meet the PS2 Portable: an open-source handheld pieced together with a custom motherboard featuring original PS2 silicon and modern niceties such as hall-effect joysticks. It can play any PS2 game natively for up to 4.5 hours on a single charge, which happens via USB-C.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A modder by the name of tschicki has built a custom "PlayStation 2 Portable" from scratch, powered by a reverse-engineered motherboard that features original PS2 silicon. The hardware sits inside a 3D-printed shell with proper controls, USB-PD charging, and a bespoke cooling solution. The project is entirely open-source and available on GitHub for anyone to follow along with, if you have the right skills and patience required.Work on the PS2 Portable actually began way back in 2022, but it was only made public last year. The four-year development period really shows when you take a look at how polished the finished product is. The design is inspired by modern handhelds such as the ROG Ally; it has an ergonomic shape with large grips that house two 5000mAh batteries to enable up to 4.5 hours of playtime on a single charge.On the front, there's an asymmetric control scheme that actually features hall-effect joysticks and face buttons from the PS Vita 2000 model. There's a blue accent piece running along the top, enveloping the triggers and the USB-C port, providing some variety in an otherwise minimalist aesthetic. You'll also find vents up there for exhaust. The rear is largely plain with a few threaded screws and a cutout with a grill for intake.Latest Videos FromImage 1 of 2(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)The entire assembly is broken down into just two parts: a top half and a bottom half that screw into each other. The cooling is handled by a custom heatsink solution that uses the fan from the Switch Lite to keep the ICs under control.This transitions us nicely into the actual crux of the project. The modder took six original ICs from the SCPH-7900x or SCPH-9000x PS2 models and put them on a custom motherboard.Built upon the existing work of community veterans who have mapped out PS2 mainboards over the years, Tschicki reverse-engineered the entire circuitry of the console. They were then able to manually draw up a new motherboard layout that integrates the aforementioned original chips with completely new traces, video routing, and power management.The donor ICs handle the bulk of the processing, such as the Emotion Engine (EE) and the Graphics Synthesizer (GS), while RP2040 microcontrollers are responsible for secondary tasks like thermals, controls, sound, and more. The modder used a custom FPGA-based video processor to get direct video out from the GS instead of going through an analog-to-digital converter that'd otherwise degrade the quality.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)As such, the PS2 portable supports many resolutions, but it's wired up to a 5" 480x800p IPS LCD running at 60 Hz. To ensure the controls work on this screen, the RP2040 is utilized to convert data from the buttons and joysticks into native DualShock 2 signals, with full rumble support. Another RP2040 monitors the battery and controls power, allowing for fast charging or "charge and play" at 5V, 9V, or 12V.Finally, to actually use the console, tschicki customized the original boot ROM to directly launch into homebrew, from where you can use the two microSD card slots present on the device to access your games. One slot hooks directly to the PS2 Memory Card's data lines and tricks the system into thinking one of those bulky 8MB Memory Cards is plugged in. You can use this for boot files and game saves, for instance.The other slot bypasses the PS2's memory controller entirely and uses the open-source MX4SIO routing standard that doesn't try to emulate a memory card. Instead, it relies on custom homebrew apps running on the console to talk directly to the SD card's native storage interface. Once the custom MX4SIO driver is initialized, you can load up any game you want, which means this slot is better for library storage.This is possible because the BIOS chip from the actual PS2 is one of the six ICs lifted from the original console, along with the CPU+GPU, the RAM (two separate chips), the I/O processor, and the SP2 audio processor. Anyhow, after the games are loaded, the handheld can play PS2 titles natively via custom software like OPL (Open PS2 Loader) or NHDDL, and supports PS1 games via DKWDRV. There is no emulation happening here.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)(Image credit: tschicki on GitHub)Because of the sheer stature of custom modding work done, the PS2 Portable is one of the most impressive community projects in recent times. The fact that everything from the 3D printer files (STLs), the PCB schematics, the FPGA code, and the software firmware is open source is just the cherry on top. You can build your own identical handheld, but it's not going to be easy, and Tschicki even warns against it. All of the relevant resources are available on GitHub, including the BOM (bill of materials) for the mechanical assembly and each PCBA. There's a 10-part documentation with steps you can follow, but they're incredibly detailed and only meant for experts with high-level soldering and programming knowledge. Do not attempt this as a fun DIY project, but then again, curiosity killed the cat.Now, technically speaking, a portable PS2 already exists — it's called the PlayStation Portable (PSP), and it was one of the most successful consoles of all time. But it doesn't have the PS2's massive catalog of classics, so there's an argument to be made there. That being said, emulation has come a long way, and your phone can even play AAA PC games now, so stick to emulation if you're only after the nostalgia and not the microsoldering anguish. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 爱好者将逆向工程版PS2塞进掌机，设计定制主板——定制的“PlayStation 2 Portable”将现代功能与原始芯片相结合
history.descriptionZh:
一位名为tschicki的Modder从零打造了一台"PlayStation 2 Portable"掌机，其核心是一块经过逆向工程设计的定制主板，搭载了原装PS2芯片。整机包裹在3D打印外壳中，配备完整的操控方案、USB-PD充电以及独特的散热系统。该项目完全开源，所有文件已上传至GitHub，供有足够技能和耐心的玩家参考制作。

这台PS2掌机的开发始于2022年，但直到去年才公开亮相。四年的研发周期从最终成品的精致程度可见一斑。设计灵感源自ROG Ally等现代掌机，采用符合人体工学的造型，两侧大型握把内各塞入一块5000mAh电池，单次充电可提供长达4.5小时的游戏时间。正面采用非对称布局，配备了霍尔效应摇杆和来自PS Vita 2000型号的按键模组。顶部贯穿一条蓝色装饰件，包裹着扳机键和USB-C接口，在极简美学中增添了一抹亮色。上方还设有排风散热口。背面较为平整，有几颗螺纹螺丝和一个进风格栅开孔。

整机仅由上下两半外壳通过螺丝组装而成。散热方案采用定制的散热片，结合Switch Lite的散热风扇来控制芯片温度。这项设计自然引出了项目的核心：Modder从SCPH-7900x或SCPH-9000x型号的PS2上拆下了六颗原始IC，将它们重新安置在一块定制主板上。

基于社区资深玩家多年绘制PS2主板布局的成果，Tschicki对整个PS2电路进行了逆向工程，然后手动绘制出新的主板布局，将这些原装芯片与全新的走线、视频路由和电源管理电路整合在一起。捐赠的IC负责大部分核心处理任务，例如情感引擎（EE）和图形合成器（GS），而RP2040微控制器则负责次级任务，如散热、控制、音频等。Modder还使用基于FPGA的定制视频处理器直接从GS输出视频信号，避免了传统模拟转数字转换器带来的画质损失。

PS2掌机支持多种分辨率，但屏幕是一块5英寸480×800p的IPS LCD面板，刷新率60Hz。为确保操控与屏幕协同工作，RP2040将按键和摇杆的数据转换为原生的DualShock 2信号，并完整支持震动反馈。另一颗RP2040负责监控电池并控制电源，支持5V、9V或12V的快充以及边充边玩功能。

最后，为了让机器实际运行，Tschicki定制了原始启动ROM，直接引导进入自制系统（homebrew）。设备配备了两个microSD卡槽：一个卡槽直接连接到PS2记忆卡的数据线，让系统误以为插入了传统8MB记忆卡，可用于存放引导文件和游戏存档；另一个卡槽则完全绕过PS2的内存控制器，采用开源的MX4SIO路由标准——它不试图模拟记忆卡，而是依赖运行在主机上的自制软件直接与SD卡的原生存储接口通信。一旦初始化了定制MX4SIO驱动，即可加载任何想要的游戏，因此这个卡槽更适合存放游戏库。

由于从原装PS2上拆下的六颗IC包括CPU+GPU、两块RAM芯片、I/O处理器、SP2音频处理器以及BIOS芯片，游戏加载后，这台掌机可通过OPL（Open PS2 Loader）或NHDDL等定制软件原生运行PS2游戏，并通过DKWDRV支持PS1游戏——这是一台不依赖模拟器的纯硬件掌机。

鉴于如此庞大的自定义改造工作量，这台PS2 Portable堪称近年来最令人印象深刻的社区项目之一。而所有3D打印文件（STL）、PCB原理图、FPGA代码以及软件固件全部开源，更是锦上添花。你可以复刻一台完全相同的手持设备，但这绝非易事——Tschicki甚至警告不要轻易尝试。所有相关资源（包括机械组装和每块PCBA的物料清单BOM）都已上传至GitHub，同时附有10个步骤的详细文档，但内容极其专业，仅适合拥有高阶焊接和编程知识的专家。请不要将其视为一个有趣的DIY项目——好奇会害死猫。

从技术上说，便携PS2早已存在——那就是PlayStation Portable（PSP），史上最成功的游戏机之一。但PSP无法运行PS2庞大的经典游戏库，因此这台掌机的价值不言而喻。当然，模拟技术也已今非昔比，连手机都能玩AAA级PC游戏了。如果你只是追求怀旧而非微焊接的痛苦，还是乖乖用模拟器吧。
